content: Kelp is rich in iodine, which the thyroid needs to convert T4 into T3.
Seaweeds like kelp contain high levels of minerals, particularly iodine, due to the water environment.
Iodine affects thyroid peroxidase (TPO), downregulating its activity and potentially increasing autoimmune attacks on the thyroid.
A study suggests balancing iodine intake with selenium to maintain selenocyine levels in the thyroid.
For optimal thyroid health, start with a low dose of kelp and monitor body temperature changes.
